channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Meta vs. Federal Trade Commission trial begins Monday. What to know
item.description: The FTC alleges Meta violated competition laws when purchasing Instagram and WhatsApp, in 2012 and 2014 respectively.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Turkey Begins Restorations of Hagia Sophia's Historic Domes
item.description: Turkey begins restorations of the nearly 1,500-year-old Hagia Sophia in Istanbul, focusing on preserving the monument's historic domes from the threat of earthquakes.
item.dc:creator: Robert Badendieck And Mehmet Guzel, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Inside ‘Signalgate’: How The Atlantic navigated a national security scandal
item.description: When government officials accidentally included Jeffrey Goldberg, The Atlantic’s editor-in-chief, in a Signal group chat discussing U.S. military plans, all hell broke loose.
item.dc:creator: Robert Safian, Fast Company

item.title: AI humanoid robot learns to mimic human emotions and behavior
item.description: A groundbreaking robot that's like a real-life Wall-E uses advanced artificial intelligence to replicate natural gestures and deliberate actions with striking accuracy.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Via porn, gore and ultra-violence, extremist groups are sinking hooks online into the very young
item.description: PARIS (AP) — After his arrest, the boy's mother was stunned to discover that her 12-year-old had been learning how to kill and gorging on videos of decapitation and torture so gruesome they made even case-hardened French court officials look away.
item.dc:creator: JOHN LEICESTER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Japanese leader says he hopes Osaka expo will help reunite a divided world
item.description: OSAKA, Japan (AP) — Japanese Prime Minister Shigeru Ishiba, speaking at Saturday's opening ceremony for the Osaka expo, expressed his hope that the event will help restore global unity in a world plagued by conflicts and trade wars.
item.dc:creator: AYAKA McGILL and MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Trump exempts phones, computers, other electronics from reciprocal tariffs
item.description: The Trump administration is exempting electronics such as phones, computers and other electronics from the "reciprocal" tariffs imposed on other nations, including China.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: Musk 'most unlikeable person' in US: House Democrat
item.description: Rep. Robert Garcia (D-Calif.) called Elon Musk the “most unlikeable person” in the U.S. during a Thursday speech in support of his party’s push to oust the tech giant from his White House role by May 30.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Theranos fraudster Elizabeth Holmes keeps fighting, claims appeal judges were confused, illogical and factually mistaken
item.description: The jury in Holmes’ trial heard evidence that she doctored internal Theranos documents and that she and Theranos had falsely suggested to investors that her machines were in battlefield use.
item.dc:creator: Ethan Baron, The Mercury News

item.title: Google Cloud Next 2025: News and insights
item.description: Google Cloud Next ’25, which runs from April 9-11 in Las Vegas is highlighting the latest advancements and future directions of Google Cloud Platform and cloud computing.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Microsoft’s latest attempt to persuade upgrades to Windows 11 falls spectacularly flat on its face
item.description: “You’ve heard the buzz about Windows 11, but is it worth the upgrade?” Microsoft asks. And going by this new article, it isn’t really.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Amazon CEO says it has to operate like the “world's largest startup”, urges AI investment
item.description: Jassy sees seven commonalities between Amazon and startups as he spells out the future for the company.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: 10 new Apple TV+ shows and movies are coming in mid-2025 – here are the 3 I'll be streaming on my Apple TV 4K
item.description: There's lots of great stuff to stream on Apple TV+ in 2025 – but some big names are missing from its latest lineup too.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Razer PC Remote Play lets you stream games to your iPhone – and it could convince me to switch from my gaming PC
item.description: Razer’s PC Remote Play game streaming service lets you play PC games on your iPhone, iPad or Android device.
item.dc:creator: alexblake.techradar@gmail.com (Alex Blake), TechRadar

item.title: Russian hackers hit military mission in Ukraine with info-stealing malware on external drives
item.description: GammaSteel infostealer found on infected devices belonging to a Western military operation in Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Google to offer its Workspace suite to the US government at a 71% discount
item.description: In a move that could reshape public-sector IT procurement, Google has entered into a landmark agreement with the US General Services Administration (GSA), offering its Workspace productivity suite to all federal agencies at discounts of up to 71%.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Teen goes from 10 nightly seizures to zero with brain implant
item.description: The NeuroOne OneRF Ablation System is a new device with FDA-cleared technology designed for both diagnosing and treating neurological disorders in one procedure.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The problem with Google’s Pixel 9a
item.description: Let’s get one thing out of the way first: Google’s Pixel 9a is one heck of a device. I’ve had the latest Pixel gizmo in my man-claws for a couple o’ weeks now, thanks to a loaner review unit provided by Google.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Arm backs big data center ambitions with migration tools
item.description: Arm Holdings expects its architecture will account for half of the data center CPU market share by the end of this year, and to help achieve those goals it has launched a batch of new migration tools for application porting.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: A quick win for DOGE? Make procurement competitive again
item.description: If DOGE is looking for a high-value, high-return target, the software procurement process is an easy win. The post A quick win for DOGE? Make procurement competitive again first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: Hackers breach Morocco's social security database in an unprecedented cyberattack
item.description: RABAT, Morocco (AP) — Morocco 's social security agency said troves of data were stolen from its systems in a cyberattack this week that resulted in personal information being leaked on the messaging app Telegram.
item.dc:creator: SAM METZ, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Infoblox, Google Cloud partner to protect hybrid and multicloud enterprise resources
item.description: Infoblox and Google Cloud announced a partnership that powers new products from each company that they say will help enterprise organizations accelerate their cloud adoption with advanced networking and security capabilities.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: OpenAI countersues Elon Musk in legal dispute over ChatGPT maker's business ambitions
item.description: OpenAI is suing Elon Musk for unfair competition and interfering with its business relationships with investors and customers, escalating a legal battle between the ChatGPT maker and the billionaire who helped bankroll the artificial intelligence startup a decade ago.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: DSPy: An open-source framework for LLM-powered applications
item.description: The past year has seen explosive growth in generative AI and the tools for integrating generative AI models into applications. Developers are eager to harness large language models (LLMs) to build smarter applications, but doing so effectively remains challenging.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Microsoft says it's 'slowing or pausing' some AI data center projects, including $1B plan for Ohio
item.description: Microsoft said it is “slowing or pausing” some of its data center construction, including a $1 billion project in Ohio, the latest sign that the demand for artificial intelligence technology that drove a massive infrastructure expansion might not need quite as many powerful computers as expected.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: New federal data threats demand new mitigation technologies
item.description: Gina Scinta, the deputy CTO at Thales Trusted Cyber Technologies, says new survey data provides insights for how agencies better secure data and networks. The post New federal data threats demand new mitigation technologies first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: OPM digitizes federal retirement booklets as part of longtime modernization effort
item.description: OPM said the digital versions of four federal retirement booklets are immediately available for download and should reduce delays for federal retirees. The post OPM digitizes federal retirement booklets as part of longtime modernization effort first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Drew Friedman, Federal News Network

item.title: Fortinet embeds AI capabilities across Security Fabric platform
item.description: Fortinet has expanded support for AI across its core cybersecurity platform to protect enterprise customers from evolving threats and simplify network and security operations.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Agentic AI – Ongoing coverage of its impact on the enterprise
item.description: Over the next few years, agentic AI is expected to bring not only rapid technological breakthroughs, but a societal transformation, redefining how we live, work and interact with the world. And this shift is happening quickly.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Top network and data center events 2025
item.description: Ready to travel to gain hands-on experience with new networking and infrastructure tools? Tech conferences – in person and virtual – give attendees a chance to access product demos, network with peers, earn continuing education credits, and catch a celebrity keynote or live entertainment.
item.dc:creator: Network World
